examples: Some people are so terribly insincere you can never tell if they are telling the truth.
All too often such policies are an insincere form of virtue signaling that benefits only the most privileged and does little to help average people.
He is above the insincere flattery.
If he's insincere, then he's little more than a dissembler trying to advance his career.
In the Western world, too little eye contact is interpreted as being impolite, insincere, or even dishonest.
